title: [CI:DOCS] UPDATE MANPAGE_SYNTAX (commit,attach,auto-update)
body:
Updated version for the MANPAGE_SYNTAX and adaption of the syntax for
the manpages of podman-commit, podman-attach, and podman-auto-update.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Procyhon*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@Procyhon Thanks this is great, but Please review the man page that is generated.  Most users of Podman are going to be using the man page, rather then viewing them in markdown, since go-md2man is not perfect, we need to make sure that the generated man pages still look good, and do not have embeded links viewable to the user.  In most cases I would prefer the markdown to be worse then make the man pages look bad.

> @Procyhon Thanks this is great, but Please review the man page that is generated. Most users of Podman are going to be using the man page, rather then viewing them in markdown, since go-md2man is not perfect, we need to make sure that the generated man pages still look good, and do not have embeded links viewable to the user. In most cases I would prefer the markdown to be worse then make the man pages look bad.

@rhatdan I had a look at the manpages in relation to the markdown and I have to agree that go-md2man is far from optimal. Is there another way to result this. Maybe one structure for md and one for the manpages. Tho this would result in each command having two files. But I think this is not the intention. Until there is a solution, I'll leave things like line breaks and links out of the files, since go-md2man can't work with them.
I think we can work on preprocessing the markdown with sed, or open up PR for go-md2man, but I am not sure how active that project is at this point.

I have no idea why the links don't work. If I use go-md2man myself on the existing podman.1.md it gives me the wrong output in which you are able to see the links in bad formatting. If I look in the existing manpage for podman.1.md, the links are not visible, which is strange, since I used the same source code. Maybe there was something changed in the way that go-md2man acts with links since the podman.1.md file was last changed.
Applied all changes. Should be ready now.
